lines.english: If I say now that my trust is in You alone, Lord,
- countless liberations knock at his door. ||1||
Gauree, Kabeer Jee:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕੈ ਹਰਿ ਸਾ ਠਾਕੁਰੁ ਭਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: jaa kai har saa tthaakur bhaaee |
lines.english: One who has the Lord as his Master, O Siblings of Destiny
why should He not cherish you also? ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਬ ਕਾਹੂ ਕਾ ਕਵਨੁ ਨਿਹੋਰਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: tab kaahoo kaa kavan nihoraa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: then what obligation do I have to anyone else? ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੀਨਿ ਲੋਕ ਜਾ ਕੈ ਹਹਿ ਭਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: teen lok jaa kai heh bhaar |
lines.english: He bears the burden of the three worlds;

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਬਸੁ ਜਉ ਬਿਖੁ ਦੇ ਮਹਤਾਰੀ ॥੩॥੨੨॥
lines.translit: kiaa bas jau bikh de mahataaree |3|22|
lines.english: If the mother poisons her own child, what can anyone do? ||3||22||
Gauree, Kabeer Jee:
Says Kabeer, through contemplation, I have obtained this one understanding.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤ ਸਤੀ ਹੋਇ ਕੈਸੇ ਨਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: bin sat satee hoe kaise naar |
lines.english: Without Truth, how can the woman be a true satee - a widow who burns herself on her husband's funeral pyre?
Without love, how can one's affection increase?
O Pandit, O religious scholar, see this and contemplate it within your heart. ||1||
One who, in his own soul, believes the Queen Maya to be true,

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਰਮਯੇ ਕਉ ਮਿਲੈ ਨ ਸੁਪਨੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: so ramaye kau milai na supanai |2|
lines.english: does not meet the Lord, even in dreams. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਬ ਲਗੁ ਰਸੁ ਤਬ ਲਗੁ ਨਹੀ ਨੇਹੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jab lag ras tab lag nahee nehu |1| rahaau |
lines.english: As long as there is attachment to pleasure, there can be no spiritual love. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਨੁ ਮਨੁ ਧਨੁ ਗ੍ਰਿਹੁ ਸਉਪਿ ਸਰੀਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: tan man dhan grihu saup sareer |
lines.english: One who surrenders her body, mind, wealth, home and self
Gauree, Kabeer Jee:
- she is the true soul-bride, says Kabeer. ||3||23||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੇ ਨਰ ਨਾਵ ਚਉੜਿ ਕਤ ਬੋੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: re nar naav chaurr kat borree |
lines.english: O man, why have you wrecked your boat and sunk it?
The whole world is engrossed in corruption.
This corruption has drowned entire families. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਕਟਿ ਨੀਰੁ ਪਸੁ ਪੀਵਸਿ ਨ ਝਾਗਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: nikatt neer pas peevas na jhaag |2|
lines.english: The water is near at hand, but the beast does not drink it in. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸਿਉ ਤੋੜਿ ਬਿਖਿਆ ਸੰਗਿ ਜੋੜੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: har siau torr bikhiaa sang jorree |1| rahaau |
lines.english: You have broken with the Lord, and joined hands with corruption. ||1||Pause||
Angels and human beings alike are burning in the raging fire.
That water is immaculate and pure, says Kabeer. ||3||24||
By constant contemplation and awareness, the water is brought forth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਹ ਕੁਲਿ ਪੂਤੁ ਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਬੀਚਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: jih kul poot na giaan beechaaree |
lines.english: That family, whose son has no spiritual wisdom or contemplation
Gauree, Kabeer Jee:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਹ ਨਰ ਰਾਮ ਭਗਤਿ ਨਹਿ ਸਾਧੀ ॥
lines.translit: jih nar raam bhagat neh saadhee |
lines.english: That man who has not practiced devotional worship of the Lord
- why didn't his mother just become a widow? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁਚੁ ਮੁਚੁ ਗਰਭ ਗਏ ਕੀਨ ਬਚਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: much much garabh ge keen bachiaa |
lines.english: So many pregnancies end in miscarriage - why was this one spared?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨਮਤ ਕਸ ਨ ਮੁਓ ਅਪਰਾਧੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: janamat kas na muo aparaadhee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: - why didn't such a sinful man die at birth? ||1||Pause||
Beautiful and handsome people are just ugly hunch-backs. ||3||25||
He lives his life in this world like a deformed amputee. ||2||
Says Kabeer, without the Naam, the Name of the Lord,
Gauree, Kabeer Jee:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਨਿਰਮਲੁ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: so niramal niramal har gun gaavai |
lines.english: Those who sing the Glorious Praises of the Pure Lord are pure.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਜਨ ਲੇਹਿ ਖਸਮ ਕਾ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jo jan lehi khasam kaa naau |
lines.english: I am forever a sacrifice to those humble beings
Who take the Name of their Lord and Master. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਕੀ ਪਗ ਪੰਕਜ ਹਮ ਧੂਰਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: tin kee pag pankaj ham dhoor |2|
lines.english: Whose hearts are filled with the All-pervading Lord. ||2||
I am the dust of the lotus feet of those

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਭਾਈ ਮੇਰੈ ਮਨਿ ਭਾਵੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: so bhaaee merai man bhaavai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: They are my Siblings of Destiny, so dear to my heart. ||1||Pause||
Slowly, steadily, Kabeer chants the Glories of God. ||3||26||
I am a weaver by birth, and patient of mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਚਿ ਮਹਾ ਰਸੁ ਤਨੁ ਭਇਆ ਕਾਠੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: sanch mahaa ras tan bheaa kaatthee |1|
lines.english: I have gathered in this most sublime essence, making my body into firewood. ||1||
Gauree, Kabeer Jee:
From the Sky of the Tenth Gate, the nectar trickles down, distilled from my furnace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੀਵਤ ਰਾਮ ਰਸੁ ਗਿਆਨ ਬੀਚਾਰਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: peevat raam ras giaan beechaaraa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: who drinks in the juice of the Lord's essence, contemplating spiritual wisdom. ||1||Pause||
He alone is called intoxicated with intuitive peace and poise,
Intuitive poise is the bar-maid who comes to serve it.
I pass my nights and days in ecstasy. ||2||
Through conscious meditation, I linked my consciousness with the Immaculate Lord.
Says Kabeer, then I obtained the Fearless Lord. ||3||27||
The natural tendency of the mind is to chase the mind.
Gauree, Kabeer Jee:
